finding: Project vocabulary now separates implementation, scoped verification, provider deployment, target-environment runtime outcome, and peer triggering into independently evidenced propositions.

observed: Repository rules forbid reporting implementation without evidence.
Current queue distinguishes source, deployment identity, and target-device runtime evidence.
Trigger records describe scheduling intent or acceptance, not peer execution.
inferred: Unqualified success verbs are a recurrent source of continuity drift unless bound to an evidence layer.
proposed: Use normalized peer-trigger phrasing and always name the verifier or observation scope.
superseded: Loose narration in which committed code could stand in for deployment or runtime proof.
unresolved: Latest Vercel deployment identity for the audio slice

contradictions_resolved: Implemented mechanism and unresolved outcome coexist at different evidence layers.
Accepted peer reschedule and unconfirmed peer execution are compatible claims.

privacy_boundary.included: Public repository artifacts, commit metadata, abstract terminology, and project-state relations.
privacy_boundary.excluded: Private chat text, health and pet-health material, credentials, identifying personal data, and unreviewed uploads.

verification.method: Fetch committed artifact and confirm valid JSON, five evolution records, explicit provenance, evidence strength, claim state, decision boundary, and review route.
